title: Chlorophyll Extraction and Spectral Analysis with Spectrometer Calibration
author: Victor vmr Rodriguez
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8eahtae

steps.instruction: Weigh out 0.25 g of sample subject and add it to a pestle.
Weigh out 0.13 g of Magnesium sulfate, heptahydrate, ACS Bio Basic Inc. Catalog #MB0329.SIZE.2.5Kg and add it to a pestle.
Add 1.0 mL of Acetone Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #34850 to the pestle.
Grind the entire mixture until the sample is consistent and the Magnesium sulfate, heptahydrate, ACS Bio Basic Inc. Catalog #MB0329.SIZE.2.5Kg is completely dissolved into the sample paste.
Add the paste into a 40 ml beaker and wash out the pestle with 1.0 mL of Acetone Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #34850 and empty it into the beaker.
Add 13.0 mL of Acetone Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #34850 to the 40 ml beaker and mix the sample thoroughly, ensuring to press and remix the sample several times.
Let the sample beaker stand for 15 minutes.
Filter the sample into a 20 ml test tube using a 20 micron retention filter paper.
Let the sample stand for a further 10 minutes.
Add 200 µL of the sample to two PCR tubes and label them according to your needed labeling system. the two tubes will serve as double tests for verification.
Centrifuge the two samples to 1200 g to settle out any particulates for 1 minute.
From each PCR tube, transfer 100 µL of the sample from the top to another PCR Tube.
Place each tube in the spectrometer.
Take to log base 10 of the absorbance percentage of the wavelength 647 nm and 664.5 nm and denote them as A 647 and A 664.

To calculate the concentration... CHL A =20.47A 647 - 4.73A 664 CHL B = 12.63A 664 - 2.52A 647 CHL Total = CHL A + CHL B Divide each concentration by 1000, multiply by the acetone used, then divide by the sample mass to get the mg/g of sample Extinction Coefficients of Chlorophyll a and b in N,N-Dimethylformamide and 80% Acetone.. Plant Physiology, vol. 77, no. 2, Jan. 1985.
Confirm readings match to within an acceptable margin of error.
Add 100 µL of Acetone Bio Basic Inc. Catalog #AC1200.SIZE.1L to a PCR tube and label it "base"
Use the "base" PCR tube to calibrate the spectrometer.
